channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Apache ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197 Added to CISA KEV Amid Active Exploitation
item.description: A recently disclosed high-severity security flaw in Apache ActiveMQ Classic has come under active exploitation in the wild, per the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA). To tha...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Operation PowerOFF identifies 75k DDoS users, takes down 53 domains
item.description: The latest wave of "Operation PowerOFF," on April 13, 2026, targeted the distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) ecosystem and its users across 21 countries. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ZionSiphon malware designed to sabotage water treatment systems
item.description: A new malware called ZionSiphon, specifically designed for operational technology, is targeting water treatment and desalination environments to sabotage their operations. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Microsoft Defender “RedSun” zero-day PoC grants SYSTEM privileges
item.description: A researcher known as "Chaotic Eclipse" has published a proof-of-concept exploit for a second Microsoft Defender zero-day, dubbed "RedSun," in the past two weeks, protesting how the company works with...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: North Korea Uses ClickFix to Target macOS Users' Data
item.description: Sapphire Sleet uses fake job offers and phony Zoom updates to deliver ClickFix attacks that steal credentials and sensitive data from Macs....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: 'Harmless' Global Adware Transforms Into an AV Killer
item.description: A benign looking update Dragon Boss pushed out in March 2025 established persistence via scheduled tasks and arranged for future payloads to be excluded from Windows Defender....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Newly Discovered PowMix Botnet Hits Czech Workers Using Randomized C2 Traffic
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of an active malicious campaign that's targeting the workforce in the Czech Republic with a previously undocumented botnet dubbed PowMix since at least December 2...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hackers exploit Marimo flaw to deploy NKAbuse malware from Hugging Face
item.description: Hackers are exploiting a critical vulnerability in Marimo reactive Python notebook to deploy a new variant of NKAbuse malware hosted on Hugging Face Spaces. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Two-Factor Authentication Breaks Free from the Desktop
item.description: Threat actors know how to bypass security systems outside of traditional IT environments. Implementing 2FA could provide a needed extra security barrier in the physical world....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Google expands Gemini AI use to fight malicious ads on its platform
item.description: Google says it is increasingly using its Gemini AI models to detect and block harmful ads on its advertising platforms, as scammers and threat actors continue to evolve their tactics to evade detectio...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft's Original Windows Secure Boot Certificate Is Expiring
item.description: The Secure Boot refresh is one of the largest coordinated security maintenance efforts across the Windows ecosystem, Microsoft said. Update those PCs soon....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New ATHR vishing platform uses AI voice agents for automated attacks
item.description: A new cybercrime platform called ATHR can harvest credentials via fully automated voice phishing attacks that use both human operators and AI agents for the social engineering phase. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Most "AI SOCs" Are Just Faster Triage. That's Not Enough.
item.description: AI-powered SOC tools promise automation, but most only speed up triage instead of reducing real workload. Tines shows how real gains come from end-to-end workflows that execute actions across systems,...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Defender 0-Day, SonicWall Brute-Force, 17-Year-Old Excel RCE and 15 More Stories
item.description: You know that feeling when you open your feed on a Thursday morning and it's just... a lot? Yeah. This week delivered. We've got hackers getting creative in ways that are almost impressive if you igno...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cisco says critical Webex Services flaw requires customer action
item.description: Cisco has released security updates to patch four critical vulnerabilities, including a fixed improper certificate validation flaw in the company's cloud-based Webex Services platform that requires fu...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: [Webinar] Find and Eliminate Orphaned Non-Human Identities in Your Environment
item.description: In 2024, compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were behind 68% of cloud breaches. Not phishing. Not weak passwords. Unmanaged non-human identities that nobody was watching. For every emp...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cisco Patches Four Critical Identity Services, Webex Flaws Enabling Code Execution
item.description: Cisco has announced patches to address four critical security flaws impacting Identity Services and Webex Services that could result in arbitrary code execution and allow an attacker to impersonate an...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Data breach at edtech giant McGraw Hill affects 13.5 million accounts
item.description: The ShinyHunters extortion group has leaked data from 13.5 million McGraw Hill user accounts, stolen after breaching the company's Salesforce environment earlier this month. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hidden Passenger? How Taboola Routes Logged-In Banking Sessions to Temu
item.description: A bank approved a Taboola pixel. That pixel quietly redirected logged-in users to a Temu tracking endpoint. This occurred without the bank’s knowledge, without user consent, and without a single secur...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Obsidian Plugin Abuse Delivers PHANTOMPULSE RAT in Targeted Finance, Crypto Attacks
item.description: A "novel" social engineering campaign has been observed abusing Obsidian, a cross-platform note-taking application, as an initial access vector to distribute a previously undocumented Windows remote a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: US nationals behind DPRK IT worker 'laptop farm' sent to prison
item.description: Two U.S. nationals have been sent to prison for helping North Korean remote information technology (IT) workers to pose as U.S. residents and get hired by over 100 companies across the country, includ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft: April Windows Server 2025 update may fail to install
item.description: Microsoft is investigating an issue causing this month's KB5082063 security update to fail to install on some Windows Server 2025 systems. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: UAC-0247 Targets Ukrainian Clinics and Government in Data-Theft Malware Campaign
item.description: The Computer Emergencies Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA) has disclosed details of a new campaign that has targeted governments and municipal healthcare institutions, mainly clinics and emergency ho...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 6-Year Ransomware Campaign Targets Turkish Homes & SMBs
item.description: While enterprises breaches make more headlines, smaller incidents tend to be under-reported, if at all, allowing campaigns to last longer with less disruption....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Critical Nginx UI auth bypass flaw now actively exploited in the wild
item.description: A critical vulnerability in Nginx UI with Model Context Protocol (MCP) support is now being exploited in the wild for full server takeover without authentication. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New AgingFly malware used in attacks on Ukraine govt, hospitals
item.description: A new malware family named 'AgingFly' has been identified in attacks against local governments and hospitals that steal authentication data from Chromium-based browsers and WhatsApp messenger. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical MCP Integration Flaw Puts NGINX at Risk
item.description: Attackers can abuse the near-maximum severity flaw in nginx-ui to restart, create, modify, and delete NGINX configuration files....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: WordPress plugin suite hacked to push malware to thousands of sites
item.description: More than 30 WordPress plugins in the EssentialPlugin package have been compromised with malicious code that allows unauthorized access to websites running them. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Navigating the Unique Security Risks of Asia's Digital Supply Chain
item.description: Regulatory differences, interconnected digital ecosystems, and the rise of AI have created a complex supply chain Asian organizations must wrangle....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Signed software abused to deploy antivirus-killing scripts
item.description: A digitally signed adware tool has deployed payloads running with SYSTEM privileges that disabled antivirus protections on thousands of endpoints, some in the educational, utilities, government, and h...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
